title: Privacy Policy
intro: This policy explains how xatelier processes personal data in line with the GDPR and French law (CNIL). It covers our web design, graphic design and social media services.

scope.p1: Applies to website users, clients, prospects and anyone contacting us via forms, email or social media.

data.l1: Identity and contact data (name, email, phone) when you contact or work with us.
data.l2: Project content you provide (briefs, assets to implement).
data.l3: Technical data (IP address, browser/device info, server logs) for security and stats.
data.l4: Analytics/marketing data via cookies or similar technologies — only with your consent.
data.l5: Billing data (invoice details) when we issue accounting documents.

sources.p1: Directly from you (forms, email, calls, social media) and from your use of our site (cookies/analytics — if you consent).

purposes.l2: Preparing proposals and performing a contract (web/graphics/social).
purposes.l3: Service maintenance and security (logs, abuse prevention).

legal.l1: Art. 6(1)(b) GDPR — contract performance or pre-contractual steps.
legal.l2: Art. 6(1)(f) GDPR — legitimate interests (security, B2B comms, claims).
legal.l4: Art. 6(1)(c) GDPR — legal obligation (e.g., accounting records).

cookies.p1: We use cookies and similar tech (e.g., Google Analytics) to analyse and improve the service. Analytics/marketing cookies run only after you give consent in the cookie banner, which you can withdraw at any time.

processors.p1: We rely on trusted providers (hosting, analytics, domains, social tools). They process data under our instructions and a data processing agreement.
processors.l2: Domains/mail providers — domain and email services.
processors.l3: Analytics (e.g., Google Analytics) — stats, only with consent.
processors.l4: Social/marketing tools — only if you use them or consent.
processors.p2: The list of processor categories may expand to essential integrations (e.g., invoicing systems) to provide services.

transfers.p1: If data is transferred outside the EEA, we ensure appropriate safeguards (e.g., Standard Contractual Clauses).

retention.l1: Enquiries and correspondence: up to 24 months from last contact.
retention.l2: Project/contract data: for the project term and up to 5 years afterwards (claims).
retention.l3: Accounting documents: up to 10 years, per law.
retention.l4: Cookies/analytics: per banner/browser settings or until consent is withdrawn.

security.p1: We apply technical and organisational measures appropriate to the risk (access control, encrypted transport, data minimisation).

minors.p1: Our services are not directed to persons under 15. We do not knowingly collect children’s data.

rights.l1: Access and a copy of your data.
rights.l6: Withdraw consent at any time (without affecting prior processing lawfulness).

exercise.p1: Contact: contact@xatelier.fr. You may also lodge a complaint with the CNIL (French data protection authority).

changes.p1: We may update this policy from time to time. The new version applies upon publication.
